images.alt: The Calvin and Hobbes strip where Calvin asks his dad to come play in the snow with him. His dad initially says no, because he has to work. Then, angry at himself, his Dad goes out to build a snowman with Calvin. As the Dad works into the night to make up for it, we see Calvin’s mum lifting him up to give one of the few, if only, kisses he ever gives his dad in the Calvin and Hobbes run. His dad is content. It’s a textless comic, and it’s lovely.

record.text: I know Calvin’s dad is already having a moment, but every terrible “outsource precious and fleeting moments with your children to tech so that you can spend more time on your work” pitch makes me think of that one strip I never really cared about as a kid, but care a lot about as an adult.

Support and Criticism For S.F. Changes
Proposed residential zoning and bullding rules for San Francis co won support last night from representatives of neighborhood groups and critical comment from speakers for the Residential Builders Association.
The split was evident at the first of three hearings scheduled by the Board of Supervisors planning housing and development commit-tee, which is considering the proposed major overhaul of the rules.
The plan is an outgrowth of a study mandated by the Planning Commission in 1974 largely because of the deluge of piecemeal de mands it received from neighborb hood groups for tougher zoning restrictions.
It would replace a planning
code passed in 1960.
Nearly 200 persons crowded into the supervisors chambers as the planning committee heard testimony from Supervisorial Districts
One, Two and Three.
Speaker after speaker repre
senting
the Richmond, Pacific
Heights, Telegraph Hill. Cow Hollow and other areas argued that the proposed ordinance developed by the Planning Department was the product of years of effort and
scores of public hearings.
and uses for landmark buildings.
The supporters said the plan was necessary to retain the family neighborhood flavor of the city.
Builders representatives
said
they'd been denied opportunities to provide sufficient "input"
to the
plan, that portions of it are based on outmoded design concepts and that the plan ignores the economic realities of housing in the city.
Vincent Walsh, president of the builders association, called the plan an attempt "by an elitist group to deny others the ability to live in San Francisco."
Speaker Samuel Schneider said additional multiple bousing is the only way to relieve the city s
housing shortage that he said causes inflated rents and costs for all housing.

"This legislation cuts out, liter-ally, low and moderate income folks. What we're doing here is pricing people out of San Francis-co," said Supervisor Robert Gon-zales. "We cannot afford to be known as the generation that saved the whales, the fishes, and the birds and the bees, but forgot its people.”

record.text: Not to be too BlueSky about this, but I’m definitely going to use the graphic in my intro Geoscience classes with the question along the lines of “Describe at least two changes you’d make to this graphic (wording or images) to better communicate differences between winter and summer solstices”.

images.alt: NOAA graphic explaining the Summer Solstice on June 21, 2026 at 4:24 a.m. ET and comparing Earth’s orientation during the June and December solstices. Two side-by-side satellite images of Earth are shown from the sun-facing side. On the left, labeled “Summer Solstice View – June,” the Northern Hemisphere is tilted toward the sun, with the equator marked below the center of the globe. On the right, labeled “Winter Solstice View – December,” the Northern Hemisphere is tilted away from the sun, with the equator appearing higher on the globe. The graphic illustrates how Earth’s tilt changes sunlight distribution between summer and winter solstices, creating the longest and shortest daylight periods of the year.

value.text: Summer solstice–the start of astronomical summer–was this morning at 4:24 a.m. EDT. The hottest parts of summer are still weeks away, but this year’s solstice does include above-normal temperatures in the Mid-Atlantic, Southern Plains, and Pacific Northwest.

images.alt: Cycle plot chart titled “Barcelona: Average temperature 1980–2025”. Twelve monthly panels show annual average temperatures from 1980 to 2025, with thin blue yearly lines and darker smoothed trends. All months show warming, with estimated linear increases ranging from +2.5°C in January and December to +6.2°C in June. The strongest increases are in late spring and summer, especially May to August.

Cycle plot titled “Região de Aveiro — Average temperature 1980–2025.” The chart shows annual average temperature for each month from 1980 to 2025, arranged from January to December in a single row of monthly segments. Each segment contains a thin blue line for yearly values and a darker smoothed trend line. The labels above the segments show estimated linear change by month: +1.4°C (Jan), +1.2°C (Feb), +0.2°C (Mar), +1.2°C (Apr), +1.5°C (May), -0.3°C (Jun), -0.6°C (Jul), -0.9°C (Aug), -0.7°C (Sep), +1.8°C (Oct), +0.6°C (Nov), and +0.9°C (Dec). The pattern suggests modest warming in most months, with slight cooling in summer months from June to September.

I don’t want to be a seer
And fly through the universe
I just want to be a man
And stay here
And let my time be open
To your time as yours is
Open to mine
And the children grow up
Inside our lives
And the house goes through
Its karma
And friends and neighbors
Successes and failures
Birds and learning
All intertwine within
The daylight and the nighttime
And I look out to see
The same tree
Differently each time

record.text:
There are almost no crustaceans in the deep ocean.

Not because they would get crushed, but because they would DISSOLVE.

The cold & high pressure cause changes to water that make it very corrosive to calcium carbonate shells.

But there's one crustacean who solves this problem with ALUMINUM ARMOR.

Tumblr screenshot of a post by abbiistabbii that reads:

The Germans really cooked making "Hobbyless behaviour" an insult. It is both devastating, applicable to a wide range of people and behaviours, and doesn't resort to swearing.

Man ranting on the internet about the Superbowl halftime show or complaining that something is "woke"? Hobbyless Behaviour. Girls mocking another girl for not looking right? Hobbyless Behaviour. Mindless vandalism? Hobbyless Behaviour.

It is more powerful than "get a life" or the English "You're Sad" because it gets to the central point of the matter, and that is wonderful. Danke, Deutsch.

A colorful, wide-view geological shaded relief map of a mountainous region with a flat region in the NNW part. The map uses a vibrant color palette to represent different rock types and ages: large flat central areas are shaded in bright yellow, while mountainous terrain is depicted in varied shades of purple, pink, magenta, and dark red. A fine grid of gray latitudinal and longitudinal lines overlays the entire map. The topography is rendered with shaded relief, giving the mountains a three-dimensional appearance with visible ridges and valleys. Small blue shapes represent water bodies, and thin black lines indicate geological boundaries or faults. A small white logo in the bottom right corner reads "HydrologyNL."

Map Features Breakdown
Yellow Shading: Typically represents Quaternary sediment deposits, which are relatively young geological formations (less than 2.6 million years old).
Purple & Pink Shading: Often indicates igneous rocks (volcanic or plutonic) or older Precambrian terrains.
Red & Magenta: Typically used for granitic rocks or other igneous intrusive bodies.
Shaded Relief: Adds a "3D" effect to the map to help visualize the physical terrain alongside the geological data.
